content.problemStatement:
Let us now review the syntax of matrix multiplication using NumPy - check out the code given in the IDE.

A similar concept to matrix multiplication is matrix inversion - matrix inversion finds a matrix that, when multiplied with the original matrix, yields the identity matrix. Only square matrices can be inverted. 
An identity matrix is a special kind of square matrix that has ones on the main diagonal (from the top left to the bottom right) and zeros in all other positions. 
```python
[[ 1 0 0]
 [ 0 1 0]
 [ 0 0 1]]
```
Check out the code given in the IDE where we compute the inverse matrix - also note that when the inverse is multiplied with the original matrix, we get the identity matrix.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
